description: Replace text in a file (find-and-replace). Returns: 'replaced in {path} ({N} replacements)'. Supports both literal string match (default) and regex matching (set 'regex: true'). When 'old' is empty: prepends 'new' at the beginning of the file. When 'new' is empty: deletes all occurrences of 'old'. With regex: use $1, $2 in 'new' to reference capture groups from 'old'. Example (literal): {"path": "main.go", "old": "fmt.Println", "new": "log.Println"}. Example (regex): {"path": "main.go", "old": "fmt\.(Print|Sprintf)", "new": "log.$1", "regex": true}. Example (prepend): {"path": "main.go", "new": "// header comment\n", "old": ""}. Example (delete): {"path": "main.go", "old": "obsolete_call()", "new": ""}
name: replace

old.description: Text to search for. If empty, 'new' is prepended at the start of the file

new.description: Replacement text. If empty, all matches of 'old' are deleted. With regex, use $1/$2 for capture groups

regex.description: If true, treat 'old' as a regex pattern; 'new' can use $1, $2 etc. for capture groups. Default: false (literal match)
